pattern: Only 26 letters exist, so instead of trying every pair of positions, try every pair of LETTERS -- for each candidate pair, pretend one occurrence of each is traded between the two words and recompute both distinct-letter counts from the frequency tables alone.
hints: You don't need to try every index pair -- there are only 26 lowercase letters, so it's enough to try every pair of letters (one that exists in word1, one that exists in word2) as the pair being traded.

Track how many times each letter appears in word1 and in word2. For a candidate trade of letter c1 (from word1) with letter c2 (from word2), you can compute what each word's distinct-letter count WOULD become without actually rebuilding either string.

A letter's count only needs to be positive to count toward the distinct total -- so after simulating the trade on the frequency tables, count how many of the 26 letters have a positive tally on each side and compare the two totals.

edge_inputs.description: both words at the minimum length, same single letter
both words at the minimum length, different letters
word1 has two distinct letters, word2 has only one
word1 has repeated letters collapsing to few distinct, word2 is all distinct letters
one word is a single repeated letter, the other already has several distinct letters
both words already have the same (large) distinct-letter count, testing whether a same-for-same trade preserves it
one word at minimum length, the other much longer but a single repeated letter

stress.description: two long random lowercase strings near the maximum length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n1 = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    n2 = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    word1 = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(n1))
    word2 = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(n2))
    return [word1, word2]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def isItPossible(self, word1, word2):
        from collections import Counter
        count1 = Counter(word1)
        count2 = Counter(word2)
        letters = [chr(ord('a') + i) for i in range(26)]
        present1 = [c for c in letters if count1[c] > 0]
        present2 = [c for c in letters if count2[c] > 0]
        for c1 in present1:
            for c2 in present2:
                new1 = dict(count1)
                new2 = dict(count2)
                new1[c1] -= 1
                new1[c2] = new1.get(c2, 0) + 1
                new2[c2] -= 1
                new2[c1] = new2.get(c1, 0) + 1
                distinct1 = sum(1 for v in new1.values() if v > 0)
                distinct2 = sum(1 for v in new2.values() if v > 0)
                if distinct1 == distinct2:
                    return True
        return False
